response.justification: This candidate is a skilled, well-rounded engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices, but is fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. The position mandates 5–10 years of professional C++ development on Windows for capital-markets systems, ideally with fixed-income or investment-banking domain expertise. This candidate's career centers on web/mobile/GIS development using primarily JavaScript/TypeScript and D, with only ~6–12 months of explicit C++ experience at National Instruments. There is no evidence of Windows-platform work, SQL Server/Oracle expertise, or any financial-services domain exposure, making them unsuitable despite transferable engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship with TDD, CI/CD practices, and clean code principles across multiple roles
Cross-domain technical versatility and proven ability to master new technologies and languages quickly
Strong communication and mentoring abilities evidenced by workshops, technical talks, and knowledge-sharing initiatives
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6–12 months explicitly mentioned) versus the mandatory 5–10 years; primary languages are JavaScript/TypeScript and D
No evidence of Windows-platform or Windows-specific system development; entire career focused on Linux, web, and cloud-based systems
Zero financial-services domain experience (capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, investment banking); background spans web, GIS, media, and data integration

key_factors.factor: C++ experience depth and Windows platform expertise
Capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge
SQL Server/Oracle and financial-systems database expertise
